data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T081749-c0e67859/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T081749-c0e67859/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T081749-c0e67859/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T081749-c0e67859/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T081749-c0e67859/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="fizeau-aedf001b">
    <title>routing: constrain harness policy auto-resolution to supported models</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
`fiz run --harness codex --policy default -p "Reply with OK." --max-iter 1 --json` routes to an unsupported model and fails before useful DDx work can start: `model "qwen3.5-27b" is not supported by harness "codex"; supported models: gpt, gpt-5, gpt-5.5, gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini`. This blocks DDx `ddx work --watch --harness codex --profile default` from using the standard policy band without an explicit model pin.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`service_execute.go:198-211` intentionally sends empty-model subprocess harness requests with Policy/MinPower through the routing engine, but the engine path is not enforcing the selected model against the subprocess harness before dispatch. `service_routing.go:1732-1745` also drops `ServiceExecuteRequest.EstimatedPromptTokens` and `RequiresTools` when constructing the `RouteRequest`, so Execute and ResolveRoute can disagree on prompt/tool capability gates. Finally, `internal/routing/gating.go:128-135` treats any explicit Harness as a full bypass of catalog power eligibility, even when Policy/MinPower are the only model-selection input and the engine is supposed to choose within that harness's eligible models.

PROPOSED FIX
Make harness+policy empty-model Execute routing produce only harness-supported concrete models before subprocess dispatch. Preserve explicit model pins and provider-routed harness semantics, but when `Harness` is set and `Model` is empty, apply policy/min-power selection within that harness's supported model set. Forward `EstimatedPromptTokens` and `RequiresTools` from `ServiceExecuteRequest` into the `RouteRequest` used by `resolveExecuteRouteWithEngine`. Add pre-dispatch validation so any engine decision for a subprocess harness fails with a typed routing error before invoking the runner if the resolved model is outside that harness allow-list.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change DDx routing policy. Do not add legacy `standard` policy aliases. Do not remove the existing explicit `--model gpt-5.4` workaround from operators; this bead is only the Fizeau routing fix.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Add `TestExecuteHarnessPolicyDefaultSelectsCodexSupportedModel` proving `resolveExecuteRoute(ServiceExecuteRequest{Harness:"codex", Policy:"default", Prompt:"x"})` returns a Codex-supported model, not `qwen3.5-27b` or any non-Codex model.
2. Add `TestResolveExecuteRouteWithEngineForwardsPromptShape` proving `EstimatedPromptTokens` and `RequiresTools` are copied from `ServiceExecuteRequest` into the `RouteRequest` path and affect routing candidates.
3. Add `TestExecuteHarnessPolicyRejectsUnsupportedEngineDecisionBeforeDispatch` proving a subprocess harness decision with an unsupported model returns a routing/validation error before the runner is invoked.
4. `fiz run --harness codex --policy default -p "Reply with OK." --max-iter 1 --json` succeeds or fails with a pre-dispatch no-viable-route error; it must not invoke Codex with `qwen3.5-27b`.
5. `go test ./internal/routing/... -run "HarnessPolicy|PromptShape|UnsupportedEngineDecision" -count=1` passes.
6. `go test . -run "TestExecuteHarnessPolicyDefaultSelectsCodexSupportedModel|TestResolveExecuteRouteWithEngineForwardsPromptShape|TestExecuteHarnessPolicyRejectsUnsupportedEngineDecisionBeforeDispatch" -count=1` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:build, area:routing, area:harness, kind:bug, downstream:ddx</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="5d1310b9aa8e4a5bb6afcc7bf86b9a8bd02465ae" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260515T081749-c0e67859"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Committed as `07a87044` with the harness-policy routing fix and the CLI follow-through.

The substantive changes are in [internal/routing/engine.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-aedf001b-20260515T081749-c0e67859/internal/routing/engine.go), [internal/routing/gating.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-aedf001b-20260515T081749-c0e67859/internal/routing/gating.go), [service_routing.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-aedf001b-20260515T081749-c0e67859/service_routing.go), [service_execute.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-aedf001b-20260515T081749-c0e67859/service_execute.go), and [agentcli/run.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-aedf001b-20260515T081749-c0e67859/agentcli/run.go). The engine now enumerates harness-supported auto-routing models for explicit harness pins with empty model, keeps power gating active for that path, forwards prompt-shape fields into execute-time routing, and validates engine-selected subprocess models before dispatch. I also added the required regressions in [service_execute_harness_pin_test.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-aedf001b-20260515T081749-c0e67859/service_execute_harness_pin_test.go), [internal/routing/gating_test.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-aedf001b-20260515T081749-c0e67859/internal/routing/gating_test.go), and a CLI request-shaping test in [agentcli/run_options_test.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-aedf001b-20260515T081749-c0e67859/agentcli/run_options_test.go).

Verification passed:
- `go test ./internal/routing/... -run "HarnessPolicy|PromptShape|UnsupportedEngineDecision" -count=1`
- `go test . -run "TestExecuteHarnessPolicyDefaultSelectsCodexSupportedModel|TestResolveExecuteRouteWithEngineForwardsPromptShape|TestExecuteHarnessPolicyRejectsUnsupportedEngineDecisionBeforeDispatch" -count=1`
- `go test ./...`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`
- CLI repro with a fake `codex` binary succeeded and logged `-m gpt-5.4-mini`; it did not invoke Codex with `qwen3.5-27b` or another inherited local model. `lefthook` also required formatting-only cleanup in three benchmark files, which is included in the commit so the repo gate passes.
